title: Biden and Xi to meet amid economic and military tensions.
content: U.S. officials said the virtual summit on Monday was intended to reassure both sides that misunderstandings would not lead to unintended clashes.
en_title: Biden and Xi to meet amid economic and military tensions.
en_content: U.S. officials said the virtual summit on Monday was intended to reassure both sides that misunderstandings would not lead to unintended clashes.

title: How a Dispute Over Groceries Led to Artillery Strikes in Ukraine
content: In a sign of volatile East-West relations, a dispute over shopping in the Ukrainian conflict zone spiraled into fighting that sent war jitters rippling through Europe and the U.S.
en_title: How a Dispute Over Groceries Led to Artillery Strikes in Ukraine
en_content: In a sign of volatile East-West relations, a dispute over shopping in the Ukrainian conflict zone spiraled into fighting that sent war jitters rippling through Europe and the U.S.

title: A Young Syrian Is Buried, as E.U. Foreign Ministers Meet on Belarus
content: A confrontation at the Polish-Belarusian border, a funeral for a dead Syrian boy trapped in between and a new round of E.U. sanctions mark the struggle with Aleksandr G. Lukashenko.

more_info.byline: By Monika Pronczuk and Steven Erlanger
des_facet: Middle East and Africa Migrant Crisis

en_title: A Young Syrian Is Buried, as E.U. Foreign Ministers Meet on Belarus
en_content: A confrontation at the Polish-Belarusian border, a funeral for a dead Syrian boy trapped in between and a new round of E.U. sanctions mark the struggle with Aleksandr G. Lukashenko.

title: A New Source of Fuel in an Aging Japan: Adult Incontinence
content: Waste from adult diapers is growing by tens of thousands of tons a year in Japan. One town may have a solution: recycle it into fuel pellets.

more_info.byline: By Motoko Rich and Makiko Inoue

en_title: A New Source of Fuel in an Aging Japan: Adult Incontinence
en_content: Waste from adult diapers is growing by tens of thousands of tons a year in Japan. One town may have a solution: recycle it into fuel pellets.

title: Austria’s new lockdown applies only to the unvaccinated.
content: The restrictions were among the toughest of a wave of new rules in Europe, which the W.H.O. has said is “back at the epicenter of the pandemic.”
en_title: Austria’s new lockdown applies only to the unvaccinated.
en_content: The restrictions were among the toughest of a wave of new rules in Europe, which the W.H.O. has said is “back at the epicenter of the pandemic.”

title: Europe Toughens Rules for Unvaccinated as Fourth Covid Wave Swells
content: Austria took the hardest line yet on Monday, beginning a lockdown aimed exclusively at those who are not inoculated, part of a pattern to make life harder for resisters.
en_title: Europe Toughens Rules for Unvaccinated as Fourth Covid Wave Swells
en_content: Austria took the hardest line yet on Monday, beginning a lockdown aimed exclusively at those who are not inoculated, part of a pattern to make life harder for resisters.

title: Cuba Crushes Dissent Ahead of Protest
content: The homes of government critics were surrounded by uniformed police, state security agents or pro-government supporters holding picket signs, human rights activists said.
en_title: Cuba Crushes Dissent Ahead of Protest
en_content: The homes of government critics were surrounded by uniformed police, state security agents or pro-government supporters holding picket signs, human rights activists said.

title: The 11th Plague: Four-Inch Scorpions Called Deathstalkers Descend on Egypt
content: Driven from underground by what seemed like a storm of biblical fury, hundreds or maybe thousands of the angry arthropods attacked humans with their storied stingers.

more_info.byline: By Vivian Yee and Nada Rashwan

en_title: The 11th Plague: Four-Inch Scorpions Called Deathstalkers Descend on Egypt
en_content: Driven from underground by what seemed like a storm of biblical fury, hundreds or maybe thousands of the angry arthropods attacked humans with their storied stingers.

content: The release was a rare positive development in the country, which has been torn by violence since a February coup.

title: Two Journalists in India Arrested as Media Crackdown Continues
content: Faced with sectarian violence in the country’s northeast, the government has responded by disputing reports on the clashes and punishing those who covered them.

more_info.byline: By Emily Schmall and Hari Kumar

en_title: Two Journalists in India Arrested as Media Crackdown Continues
en_content: Faced with sectarian violence in the country’s northeast, the government has responded by disputing reports on the clashes and punishing those who covered them.

title: Taxi Blast Outside U.K. Hospital Is Declared a Terrorist Act
content: Local leaders praised the driver as a hero, saying that he may have prevented a bigger calamity. He escaped the burning vehicle and locked the doors, trapping a passenger carrying explosives inside.
en_title: Taxi Blast Outside U.K. Hospital Is Declared a Terrorist Act
en_content: Local leaders praised the driver as a hero, saying that he may have prevented a bigger calamity. He escaped the burning vehicle and locked the doors, trapping a passenger carrying explosives inside.

title: These Afghan Students Found Refuge in Iraq
content: More than 100 students from the American University of Afghanistan fled the Taliban and are now studying in Iraq.
en_title: These Afghan Students Found Refuge in Iraq
en_content: More than 100 students from the American University of Afghanistan fled the Taliban and are now studying in Iraq.

title: In Latest Twist in Philippine Politics, President to Run for Senate
content: Rodrigo Duterte entered the race days after his daughter announced her bid for vice president. He had previously said he would leave politics at the end of his term.
en_title: In Latest Twist in Philippine Politics, President to Run for Senate
en_content: Rodrigo Duterte entered the race days after his daughter announced her bid for vice president. He had previously said he would leave politics at the end of his term.

title: They Warned Their Names Were on a Hit List. They Were Killed.
content: In less than a month, assassins have killed at least eight people in the Rohingya refugee camps of Bangladesh, silencing those who have dared to speak out against the violent gangs.

title: A Maori tribe tells anti-vaccine protesters to stop performing their haka.
content: Ngati Toa, which owns the legal rights to the dance, said it supports Covid vaccinations as the best chance to protect a group that has suffered disproportionally from pandemics.

title: Taxi Explodes Outside Liverpool Hospital, Killing One
content: Three men in their twenties were arrested under Britain’s Terrorism Act several hours after the explosion, in which a second person was injured, the authorities said.
en_title: Taxi Explodes Outside Liverpool Hospital, Killing One

title: Qaddafi Son to Run for President in Libya
content: Seif al-Islam el-Qaddafi, the former heir apparent of the deposed Libyan dictator Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi, filed his candidacy papers on Sunday for the election expected next month.

title: Afghan College Students Find a New Life, and Safety, in Iraq
content: Regarded as infidels by the Taliban, over 100 students from the American University in Kabul have found an unlikely refuge in the Iraqi Kurdish city of Sulaimaniya.
en_title: Afghan College Students Find a New Life, and Safety, in Iraq
en_content: Regarded as infidels by the Taliban, over 100 students from the American University in Kabul have found an unlikely refuge in the Iraqi Kurdish city of Sulaimaniya.

title: At Least 1 Dead After Quakes Strike Southern Iran
content: Two moderate temblors hit Hormozgan Province on Sunday afternoon, state TV reported.
en_title: At Least 1 Dead After Quakes Strike Southern Iran
en_content: Two moderate temblors hit Hormozgan Province on Sunday afternoon, state TV reported.

title: Austria’s new lockdown confines the unvaccinated to their homes.
content: The country, facing the biggest surge in virus cases since the pandemic started, is putting strict curbs on the movement of those without shots.

title: After Sierra Leone Explosion, a Health System Is Strained

title: Queen Elizabeth Misses Remembrance Service Because of Sprained Back
content: The British monarch, 95, has put off multiple trips and engagements recently, and the latest cancellation has deepened anxiety about her health. She has now been out of sight since mid-October.

title: In Cuba, Desires for Food and Freedom May Spark a Rare Day of Protest

title: What is tromethamine? It is used to stabilize Covid vaccines.
content: Also known as tris, the compound helps maintain the pH of vaccines, extending shelf life at warmer temperatures.

title: India Wants to Send Wheat to Ease Afghan Hunger Crisis. Pakistan Has Yet to OK Transit.
content: Indian officials say Islamabad is dragging its feet on a request of transit for 50,000 tons of wheat to Afghanistan, where nine million people are on the brink of starvation.

title: For Meghan, an Image Rendered in a U.K.-U.S. Split Screen
